prompt:
In the pmn_pt_shear_actuator/src/ directory of the ieee-research warehouse, create pmn_pt_shear_actuator.py and test_pmn_pt_shear_actuator.py.

Background: The d36 shear mode of a PMN-PT (lead magnesium niobate-lead titanate) piezoelectric single crystal utilizes electric field-induced shear strain to generate displacement. Compared with the traditional d33 longitudinal mode, the d36 shear mode has a higher piezoelectric coefficient (d36 > 2000 pC/N), larger free strain, and lower hysteresis. Typical applications: high-precision micro-positioning platform, fast tool servo, nanoimprinting. Core control challenges: nonlinear creep unique to shear mode, rate-dependent hysteresis, load cross-coupling, temperature sensitivity.

## pmn_pt_shear_actuator.py

### 1. ShearActuatorPlant class
- Modeling the electro-mechanical coupling behavior of PMN-PT d36 shear mode
- Parameters: d36 piezoelectric coefficient (~2000-3000 pC/N), elastic compliance coefficient s55E, dielectric constant epsilon33T, geometric dimensions (length x width x height)
- Status: shear strain gamma, strain rate gamma_dot, charge Q
- Built-in rate-dependent hysteresis model (based on Duhem or Bouc-Wen improvements)
- Creep model: logarithmic creep gamma_creep(t) = gamma_0 * log(1 + t/tau)
- simulate(voltage_seq, dt) -> displacement-time series
- get_parameters() -> current model parameters
-Supports multiple mode switching: quasi-static, medium frequency scanning, high-speed drive

### 2. ShearHysteresisCompensator class
- Feedforward compensator based on PI (Prandtl-Ishlinskii) inverse model
- Superposition operator and play operator
- Rate dependent threshold distribution
- Online identification: Recursive least squares (RLS) parameter update
- compensate(reference_displacement) -> Feedforward compensation voltage
- Can be used in parallel with feedback controller (feedforward + feedback)

### 3. ShearMotorESO class (extended status observer)
- 3rd order ESO: z1 (displacement estimate), z2 (velocity/current), z3 (total disturbance: creep + hysteresis + load + temperature)
- Parameter: Observer bandwidth w0 (typ. 100-500 rad/s)
- fal function parameters optimized for shear mode: alpha=[0.5, 0.25, 0.125], delta=0.01
- update(y_meas, u) -> state estimate update
- get_disturbance() -> Current total disturbance estimate
- get_estimated_displacement() -> Displacement after filtering

### 4. ShearADRCController class
- Integrated: tracking differentiator TD + ESO + nonlinear state error feedback NLSEF
- Parameters: wc (control bandwidth), w0 (observer bandwidth), b0 (control gain), lambda (error nonlinear coefficient)
- Anti-windup: integral anti-windup
- Creep feedforward compensation: based on logarithmic creep model
- Temperature adaptive: adjust observer gain according to temperature
- track(setpoint, y_meas, dt) -> control voltage
- Multi-mode: step mode, sine tracking mode, arbitrary trajectory mode

### 5. Optional ShearCharacterizer class
- Automatic characterization of piezoelectric hysteresis primary and secondary loops
- Parameter identification: d36, hysteresis width, creep time constant tau
- generate_characterization_waveforms() -> Characterization voltage sequence
- Output model parameters for controller initialization

## test_pmn_pt_shear_actuator.py
- Plant basic function: voltage-displacement curve (butterfly curve)
- Hysteresis compensator open loop linearization test
- ESO convergence speed test (step perturbation)
- ADRC closed-loop step response (no overshoot, steady-state accuracy < 1nm)
- Sine trajectory tracking test (1Hz, 10Hz, 100Hz)
- Sudden load immunity test (50% rated load sudden change)
- Comparison of creep compensation effects (open loop vs ADRC+creep feedforward)
- Temperature drift robustness test
- Comparison of rate-dependent hysteresis compensation effects

Technical requirements: Python 3.10+, pure NumPy, type annotations, Chinese documentation
Branch name: feat/pmnpt-shear-actuator
title: PMN-PT d36 Shear Actuator ADRC Control
